overview.introduction: Generate individualized PDF test forms for the ISO Contrast-Resolution perceptual round robin. Each page is marked with a unique QR code for traceability.
requirements: A calibrated ICC profile for your specific device, colorants, substrate, and print settings.
Your print system specifications (device, colorants, substrate, settings).
A modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, or Safari).

recommended.text: This is the current recommended version.

nonRecommended.text: A newer version is available: {{recommended}}. Older versions may produce results that are not comparable with other participants.

eightBit.text: The 8-bit variant is only used for troubleshooting on systems when generation with the 16-bit variant fails. Use the standard version unless you have a specific reason.

maps.text: The Maps variant includes only the map assets. Use the standard version for the full test form.

newVersionAvailable.text: A new test form version has been published since your last session. Your selection has been updated to the latest version.

default.text: Select the ICC profile calibrated for your exact device, colorants, substrate, and print settings.

cmyk.text: CMYK profile detected: {{description}}. Two rendering passes will be generated (Relative Colorimetric and K-Only GCR).

cmykMaxGCR.text: CMYK Max GCR profile detected: {{description}}. Single rendering pass — this profile already maximizes black generation.

rgb.text: RGB profile detected: {{description}}. The test form will be converted to this RGB color space.

gray.text: Gray profile detected: {{description}}. The test form will be converted to grayscale.

nonPrinter.text: {{description}} is a {{deviceClass}} profile, not a printer profile. PDF/X-4 output intents require a printer (prtr) profile for conformance. The test form will still be generated, but may fail preflight validation.

8-bit.text: Standard precision — suitable for most printing systems.

16-bit.text: Higher precision — use only when printing on systems with wide-gamut or high-dynamic-range. Due to browser limitations, you may need to use the separate chains

auto.text: Each element retains its source bit depth. Mixed bit depths are possible across pages.

withDocket.text: Your email and generation timestamp are embedded in the slug QR codes. Device, colorants, substrate, and settings are stored in the docket PDF only — not in the QR codes.

withoutDocket.text: Your email and generation timestamp are embedded in the slug QR codes. Device, colorants, substrate, and settings are stored in the metadata JSON only — not in the QR codes.

withDocket.text: The generator produces a docket PDF and test form PDF(s). The docket contains your ICC profile and all generation parameters. Submit the docket PDF digitally alongside your physical prints — do not print it.

withoutDocket.text: The generator produces test form PDF(s) and a metadata JSON file. Keep the metadata file alongside your test form PDF — it contains the generation parameters.

docket.explainer: This docket contains the ICC profile and all parameters used to generate your test form. Keep this file and submit it digitally alongside your physical prints. Do not print the docket.

sections.heading: Slug QR Codes
sections.content: Each page in the test form has a slug area containing a QR code that encodes:
list: Slug header — test form identifier and output profile category
Page title and variant (if applicable)
Source color space and resolution
Rendering intent and output profile name
Participant email and generation timestamp
sections.footer: The slug QR code ties each printed page back to the layout, the specific test form digital file, and its docket (which contains the ICC profile). The generation timestamp is shared across all PDFs in a session — docket and all rendering intent passes — so they can be identified as a set. This is essential for sorting scanned pages from different round-robin participants.

sections.heading: Asset Barcodes
sections.content: Assets contain embedded barcodes (QR or PDF417) independent of the slug QR codes. These identify the source asset, variant, and version.
list: ConRes 21 (CR21) — QR codes with a robust configuration and labeling system developed by Franz, including color space distinguisher (sRGB, sGray, Lab), target layout parameters (rows, columns), intended to guide automated analysis software
ConRes 20 (TV25 vs TV75) — PDF417 codes with sRGB, sGray, or Lab distinguisher, each with its own source asset
Interlaken Aerial — PDF417, same code for sRGB and sGray since they are the same source asset (sGray is a grayscale conversion of sRGB)
Interlaken Map — PDF417 with "color" or "gray" distinguisher since sRGB and sGray are different source assets
Vitznau Map (contour) — PDF417 with "color" or "gray" distinguisher since sRGB and sGray are different source assets

Pictorial: London (2 elements), Winter Trees (4 elements), Lake and Cat (2 elements) — PDF417 per element, same code irrespective of color space since they are all converted from the same source assets

Type Sizes — PDF417 per element: Palatino, Palatino Italic, Helvetica, Edwardian Script (4 elements), irrespective of color space. Note: sGray and Separation Black variants need distinguisher codes — revision pending before round-robin
Lissajou — PDF417 per element: Frames and Shapes (2 elements), irrespective of color space. Note: sGray and Separation Black variants need distinguisher codes — revision pending before round-robin

sections.heading: Barcode Hierarchy and Segmentation
sections.content: All asset-level barcodes work in tandem with the slug QR code:
list: Slug QR — identifies the page, participant, output profile, rendering intent, and generation timestamp
Asset barcode — identifies the specific source asset, variant, and version
sections.footer: Together, these enable the TestFormSegmenter to automate the segmentation process — splitting scanned pages into individual assessment targets for perceptual psychometrics testing.

sections.heading: Color Space Handling
sections.content: The test form contains assets in multiple source color spaces:
list: sRGB — standard RGB pictorial and map assets
sGray — grayscale variants (some converted from sRGB, some from separate sources)
Lab — CIE Lab color space (ConRes targets only)
SepK — Separation Black (Type Sizes and Lissajou only)
sections.footer: The generator converts all source color spaces to the output profile's color space using the selected rendering intent. For CMYK profiles without Max GCR, two passes are generated: Relative Colorimetric and K-Only GCR.

sections.heading: Output Profile Requirements
sections.content: The ICC profile must be calibrated for the exact combination of:
list: Print device (make and model)
Colorants (ink set)
Substrate (paper or media type)
Print settings (resolution, screening, quality mode)
sections.footer: A generic or mismatched profile will produce unreliable test results. The profile is embedded in the docket PDF for verification.

sections.heading: Docket PDF
sections.content: The docket is a separate PDF generated alongside the test form (introduced in F10a). It contains:
list: The ICC output profile used for generation
All generation parameters (device, colorants, substrate, settings, email)
Assembly plan details (rendering intents, page count, profile category)
Generation timestamp (shared across all PDFs in the session)
sections.footer: The docket must be kept and submitted digitally alongside physical prints. It does not need to be printed. Without the docket, submitted prints cannot be processed.

sections.heading: Legacy: Metadata JSON
sections.content: Test form versions prior to F10a generated a metadata.json file instead of a docket PDF. The metadata JSON contained the generation parameters and a base64-encoded copy of the ICC profile. Starting with F10a, the metadata is embedded inside the docket PDF — the ICC profile is no longer duplicated in the metadata because it is already embedded in the PDF as the output intent.
